title: Experiments with On-device AI — What building on Gemini Nano actually teaches you
excerpt: Chrome ships a real LLM inside the browser now — Gemini Nano, exposed through a handful of built-in...
content_html:
Chrome ships a real LLM inside the browser now — Gemini Nano, exposed through a handful of built-in JS APIs (LanguageModel, Rewriter, Proofreader, Summarizer, Writer). No API key, no network call, no per-token bill. It runs on-device, which sounds like a free lunch right up until you start building against it and discover that "on-device" changes almost every assumption you've built up shipping against OpenAI or Anthropic's APIs.

I spent the last stretch building a small Chrome extension (Quill — five writing tools, on-device AI only) as a testbed for exactly this. The extension itself isn't really the point here; it's the harness I used to find out what's actually different about building on a model that lives on the user's machine instead of yours. These are the things I'd tell another dev before they start.

There isn't one API — there's a task API and a fallback API, and you need both

Chrome exposes two layers: dedicated task APIs (Rewriter, Proofreader, Summarizer, Writer), each shaped around one job with typed options, and the general-purpose LanguageModel (the "Prompt API"), which you drive with a raw system prompt. They're gated by separate chrome://flags entries and roll out to stable independently — which means on any given real machine, some subset of them is enabled and the rest aren't.

Hard-coding against Rewriter alone means the feature silently stops working on any profile where only the Prompt API is on, or vice versa. The fix is a fallback chain per capability: try the dedicated API, and if it's unavailable, reconstruct the same task as a system prompt for LanguageModel.

async function doRewrite(sharedContext, text, lengthPref, variationHint) {
  const a = await avail('Rewriter');
  if (a !== 'unavailable') {
    const r = await Rewriter.create({ sharedContext, tone: 'as-is', length: lengthPref });
    try { return await r.rewrite(text, { context: variationHint }); }
    finally { r.destroy(); }
  }
  // Rewriter isn't enabled on this machine — do the same job via the Prompt API.
  const sys = `${sharedContext} Preserve the original meaning. Output only the rewritten text.`;
  return promptOnce(variationHint ? `${sys} ${variationHint}` : sys, text);
}

This roughly doubles the code per feature, and it's the difference between "works on my machine" and "works." Treat "the task API is enabled" as a runtime fact you check, not a build-time assumption.

"Unavailable" is a state machine, not a boolean

availability() doesn't return true/false — it returns unavailable, downloadable, downloading, or available. The first time I hit unavailable on a clean profile with the flags apparently on, I assumed it meant the API genuinely couldn't be reached from a content script's isolated world, and started building an offscreen-document relay to work around a permissions boundary that, it turned out, didn't exist. The actual cause: I'd toggled the flag but never restarted the browser, so Chrome was still running with the old flag state. unavailable and "you forgot to restart" produce an identical string.

Once you're actually in the downloadable or downloading states, create() takes a monitor callback that reports download progress — the first call to a newly-enabled API triggers a one-time model pull that can take a while, and if you don't wire this up, your UI just looks hung:

const opts = { sharedContext, tone: 'as-is' };
if (a !== 'available') {
  opts.monitor = (m) => m.addEventListener('downloadprogress', e => {
    showProgress(`Downloading model… ${Math.round(e.loaded * 100)}%`);
  });
}
const r = await Rewriter.create(opts);

Two takeaways: don't build a workaround for "unavailable" until you've confirmed it isn't just an unflipped flag or a pending download, and always assume the first real call from a fresh install is going to be slow.

The model is small enough that instructions aren't guarantees

Task APIs and the Prompt API both run a genuinely small model compared to what you're used to calling over HTTP. Concretely, that means:

  • Length and count instructions are approximate. Ask for "exactly one sentence" or "under 200 characters" and you'll get close, not exact, often enough that you can't display raw output where a hard limit matters — measure it in code and treat the model's attempt as a first draft, not ground truth.
  • "Give me three different versions" undersells how similar they'll be. A single call asking for 3 variants tends to return 3 variants of the same idea with light word-swaps. Better results come from three separate calls, each with an explicit instruction to lean a different direction (shorter, more formal, more casual) — treat variation as something you engineer via prompts, not something you request as a parameter.
  • Output language isn't implicit. Both LanguageModel and the task APIs accept expectedInputs / expectedOutputs with a languages array. Skip it and you get a console warning and (per Chrome's own docs) less reliable output attestation — it's a one-line fix that's easy to miss because nothing breaks without it, it just gets quietly worse. 
const opts = {
  initialPrompts: [{ role: 'system', content: systemPrompt }],
  expectedInputs: [{ type: 'text', languages: ['en'] }],
  expectedOutputs: [{ type: 'text', languages: ['en'] }],
};

You can't build a CI pipeline that assumes the model exists

There's no Gemini Nano on a GitHub Actions runner, and probably not on your laptop today either unless you've already downloaded it. If your test suite needs a real model, you don't have a test suite — you have a manual QA checklist. The only way to get real coverage is to mock Rewriter / Summarizer / LanguageModel etc. as jsdom globals and run your actual, unmodified feature code against the mock.

That works, with one sharp edge: your mock has to vary its output the same way the real model does, or it'll hide bugs instead of catching them. I had a tone-variant generator that deduped near-identical outputs (a legit anti-repetition guard), tested against a mock that returned the same canned string regardless of which prompt it was called with. Every "generate 3 variants" test passed — with exactly one result, because the mock made all three collapse to the same dedupe key, and an assertion checking "got at least one result" didn't notice it got the wrong number. The mock's laziness happened to produce output that looked like success. Once the mock was made to vary its response per input (the same way any real generative model does), the test correctly started failing until the dedupe logic was actually right.

General rule: if a feature fans out into N distinct things, your mock has to be capable of returning N distinct things, or you're not testing the fan-out — you're testing that your code doesn't crash.

The browser boundary is real, even if your first guess about it is wrong

A content script — the code that runs on the page you're actually operating on — cannot navigate the tab to chrome:// URLs; that's Chrome policy, not a bug to route around. If part of your UX involves sending someone to chrome://flags to enable a capability, that navigation has to be requested from the extension's background service worker instead, via chrome.tabs.create or similar, triggered by a message from the content script.

// content script: can't navigate to chrome:// itself
chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ type: 'OPEN_FLAGS', flag: 'rewriter-api-for-gemini-nano' });

// background worker: can
chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener((msg) => {
  if (msg.type === 'OPEN_FLAGS') {
    chrome.tabs.create({ url: `chrome://flags/#${msg.flag}` });
  }
});

This is the boundary I correctly suspected existed — the mistake earlier was assuming the availability check had a similar boundary when it didn't. Worth internalizing both halves: some restrictions on what a page-level script can do are real and permanent, and the fix is to hand the action to a more privileged context. Others are red herrings that look identical from the error message alone. The only way to tell them apart is to isolate the actual cause before you build the workaround.

None of this is Quill-specific — it's what building anything on Chrome's on-device AI actually involves, independent of what the feature does.

If you want to see where these came out the other end: Quill is five writing tools (rewrite, proofread, summarize, list/table, freeform compose) running entirely on Gemini Nano, free on the Chrome Web Store.

These are the things I'd tell another dev before they start.

sentences.en: Chrome exposes two layers: dedicated task APIs (Rewriter, Proofreader, Summarizer, Writer), each shaped around one job with typed options, and the general-purpose LanguageModel (the "Prompt API"), which you drive with a raw system prompt.
sentences.zh: Chrome 公开了两层：专用任务 API（重写器、校对器、摘要器、编写器），每个 API 都围绕一项具有类型化选项的作业，以及通用语言模型（“提示 API”），您可以使用原始系统提示符来驱动它。

sentences.en: They're gated by separate chrome://flags entries and roll out to stable independently — which means on any given real machine, some subset of them is enabled and the rest aren't.
sentences.zh: 它们由单独的 chrome://flags 条目进行控制，并独立稳定地推出 - 这意味着在任何给定的真实机器上，它们的某些子集已启用，而其余的则未启用。

The fix is a fallback chain per capability: try the dedicated API, and if it's unavailable, reconstruct the same task as a system prompt for LanguageModel.

sentences.en: The fix is a fallback chain per capability: try the dedicated API, and if it's unavailable, reconstruct the same task as a system prompt for LanguageModel.
sentences.zh: 修复方法是每个功能都有一个后备链：尝试专用 API，如果它不可用，则重建与 LanguageModel 的系统提示相同的任务。

sentences.en: unavailable and "you forgot to restart" produce an identical string.

sentences.en: Once you're actually in the downloadable or downloading states, create() takes a monitor callback that reports download progress — the first call to a newly-enabled API triggers a one-time model pull that can take a while, and if you don't wire this up, your UI just looks hung:
sentences.zh: 一旦您实际上处于可下载或正在下载状态，create() 就会采用一个监视器回调来报告下载进度 - 对新启用的 API 的第一次调用会触发一次性模型拉取，这可能需要一段时间，如果您不将其连接起来，您的 UI 看起来就会挂起：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

sentences.zh: 更好的结果来自三个单独的调用，每个调用都有明确的指示，以倾向不同的方向（更短、更正式、更随意）——将变化视为您通过提示设计的东西，而不是您作为参数请求的东西。

Skip it and you get a console warning and (per Chrome's own docs) less reliable output attestation — it's a one-line fix that's easy to miss because nothing breaks without it, it just gets quietly worse.

sentences.en: Both LanguageModel and the task APIs accept expectedInputs / expectedOutputs with a languages array.
sentences.zh: LanguageModel 和任务 API 都接受带有语言数组的预期输入/预期输出。

sentences.en: Skip it and you get a console warning and (per Chrome's own docs) less reliable output attestation — it's a one-line fix that's easy to miss because nothing breaks without it, it just gets quietly worse.
sentences.zh: 跳过它，你会得到一个控制台警告和（根据 Chrome 自己的文档）不太可靠的输出证明 - 这是一个很容易被忽略的单行修复，因为没有它就没有任何问题，它只会变得更糟。

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: You can't build a CI pipeline that assumes the model exists

sentences.en: You can't build a CI pipeline that assumes the model exists
sentences.zh: 您无法构建假设模型存在的 CI 管道

blocks.html_en: There's no Gemini Nano on a GitHub Actions runner, and probably not on your laptop today either unless you've already downloaded it. If your test suite needs a real model, you don't have a test suite — you have a manual QA checklist. The only way to get real coverage is to mock Rewriter / Summarizer / LanguageModel etc. as jsdom globals and run your actual, unmodified feature code against the mock.

sentences.en: There's no Gemini Nano on a GitHub Actions runner, and probably not on your laptop today either unless you've already downloaded it.
sentences.zh: GitHub Actions 运行程序上没有 Gemini Nano，今天您的笔记本电脑上也可能没有，除非您已经下载了它。

sentences.en: If your test suite needs a real model, you don't have a test suite — you have a manual QA checklist.
sentences.zh: 如果您的测试套件需要真实模型，那么您就没有测试套件 - 您有一个手动 QA 检查表。

sentences.en: The only way to get real coverage is to mock Rewriter / Summarizer / LanguageModel etc. as jsdom globals and run your actual, unmodified feature code against the mock.
sentences.zh: 获得真正覆盖的唯一方法是将 Rewriter / Summarizer / LanguageModel 等模拟为 jsdom 全局变量，并针对模拟运行实际的、未修改的功能代码。

blocks.html_en: That works, with one sharp edge: your mock has to vary its output the same way the real model does, or it'll hide bugs instead of catching them. I had a tone-variant generator that deduped near-identical outputs (a legit anti-repetition guard), tested against a mock that returned the same canned string regardless of which prompt it was called with. Every "generate 3 variants" test passed — with exactly one result, because the mock made all three collapse to the same dedupe key, and an assertion checking "got at least one result" didn't notice it got the wrong number. The mock's laziness happened to produce output that looked like success. Once the mock was made to vary its response per input (the same way any real generative model does), the test correctly started failing until the dedupe logic was actually right.

sentences.en: That works, with one sharp edge: your mock has to vary its output the same way the real model does, or it'll hide bugs instead of catching them.
sentences.zh: 这是有效的，有一个尖锐的优势：你的模拟必须像真实模型一样改变它的输出，否则它会隐藏错误而不是捕获它们。

sentences.en: I had a tone-variant generator that deduped near-identical outputs (a legit anti-repetition guard), tested against a mock that returned the same canned string regardless of which prompt it was called with.
sentences.zh: 我有一个音调变体生成器，可以删除几乎相同的输出（一个合法的防重复防护），并针对一个模拟进行测试，该模拟返回相同的固定字符串，无论使用哪个提示调用它。

sentences.en: Every "generate 3 variants" test passed — with exactly one result, because the mock made all three collapse to the same dedupe key, and an assertion checking "got at least one result" didn't notice it got the wrong number.
sentences.zh: 每一个“生成 3 个变体”测试都通过了——只有一个结果，因为模拟使所有三个都崩溃到同一个重复数据删除密钥，并且断言检查“至少得到一个结果”没有注意到它得到了错误的数字。

sentences.en: The mock's laziness happened to produce output that looked like success.
sentences.zh: 模拟的懒惰恰好产生了看起来像是成功的输出。

sentences.en: Once the mock was made to vary its response per input (the same way any real generative model does), the test correctly started failing until the dedupe logic was actually right.
sentences.zh: 一旦模拟改变了每个输入的响应（与任何真实的生成模型相同），测试就会开始失败，直到重复数据删除逻辑实际上是正确的。

blocks.html_en: General rule: if a feature fans out into N distinct things, your mock has to be capable of returning N distinct things, or you're not testing the fan-out — you're testing that your code doesn't crash.

sentences.en: General rule: if a feature fans out into N distinct things, your mock has to be capable of returning N distinct things, or you're not testing the fan-out — you're testing that your code doesn't crash.
sentences.zh: 一般规则：如果一个功能扇出为 N 个不同的事物，那么您的模拟必须能够返回 N 个不同的事物，否则您就不会测试扇出 - 您正在测试您的代码不会崩溃。

blocks.html_en: The browser boundary is real, even if your first guess about it is wrong

sentences.en: The browser boundary is real, even if your first guess about it is wrong
sentences.zh: 浏览器边界是真实的，即使你的第一次猜测是错误的

blocks.html_en: A content script — the code that runs on the page you're actually operating on — cannot navigate the tab to chrome:// URLs; that's Chrome policy, not a bug to route around. If part of your UX involves sending someone to chrome://flags to enable a capability, that navigation has to be requested from the extension's background service worker instead, via chrome.tabs.create or similar, triggered by a message from the content script.

sentences.en: A content script — the code that runs on the page you're actually operating on — cannot navigate the tab to chrome:// URLs; that's Chrome policy, not a bug to route around.
sentences.zh: 内容脚本（在您实际操作的页面上运行的代码）无法将选项卡导航到 chrome:// URL；这是 Chrome 的政策，而不是可以绕过的错误。

sentences.en: If part of your UX involves sending someone to chrome://flags to enable a capability, that navigation has to be requested from the extension's background service worker instead, via chrome.tabs.create or similar, triggered by a message from the content script.
sentences.zh: 如果您的用户体验的一部分涉及将某人发送到 chrome://flags 来启用某项功能，则必须通过 chrome.tabs.create 或类似的方式从扩展程序的后台服务工作者请求该导航，并由内容脚本中的消息触发。

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: This is the boundary I correctly suspected existed — the mistake earlier was assuming the availability check had a similar boundary when it didn't. Worth internalizing both halves: some restrictions on what a page-level script can do are real and permanent, and the fix is to hand the action to a more privileged context. Others are red herrings that look identical from the error message alone. The only way to tell them apart is to isolate the actual cause before you build the workaround.

sentences.en: This is the boundary I correctly suspected existed — the mistake earlier was assuming the availability check had a similar boundary when it didn't.
sentences.zh: 这是我正确怀疑存在的边界 - 之前的错误是假设可用性检查具有类似的边界，但事实并非如此。

sentences.en: Worth internalizing both halves: some restrictions on what a page-level script can do are real and permanent, and the fix is to hand the action to a more privileged context.
sentences.zh: 这两部分都值得内化：对页面级脚本可以执行的操作的一些限制是真实且永久的，解决方法是将操作交给更有特权的上下文。

sentences.en: Others are red herrings that look identical from the error message alone.
sentences.zh: 其他一些则只是转移注意力，仅从错误消息来看就完全一样。

sentences.en: The only way to tell them apart is to isolate the actual cause before you build the workaround.
sentences.zh: 区分它们的唯一方法是在构建解决方法之前隔离实际原因。

blocks.html_en: None of this is Quill-specific — it's what building anything on Chrome's on-device AI actually involves, independent of what the feature does.

sentences.en: None of this is Quill-specific — it's what building anything on Chrome's on-device AI actually involves, independent of what the feature does.
sentences.zh: 这些都不是 Quill 特有的——它是在 Chrome 的设备上 AI 上构建任何东西实际涉及的内容，与功能的作用无关。

blocks.html_en: If you want to see where these came out the other end: Quill is five writing tools (rewrite, proofread, summarize, list/table, freeform compose) running entirely on Gemini Nano, free on the Chrome Web Store.

sentences.en: If you want to see where these came out the other end: Quill is five writing tools (rewrite, proofread, summarize, list/table, freeform compose) running entirely on Gemini Nano, free on the Chrome Web Store.
sentences.zh: 如果你想看看这些结果是如何产生的：Quill 是五个写作工具（重写、校对、总结、列表/表格、自由格式撰写），完全在 Gemini Nano 上运行，在 Chrome 网上应用店免费。

content_html_zh:
Chrome 现在在浏览器中提供了真正的 LLM——Gemini Nano，通过一些内置 JS API（LanguageModel、Rewriter、Proofreader、Summarizer、Writer）公开。 没有 API 密钥、没有网络调用、没有按代币计费。 它在设备上运行，这听起来像是免费午餐，直到您开始针对它进行构建并发现“设备上”几乎改变了您针对 OpenAI 或 Anthropic 的 API 建立的所有假设。

我花了最后一段时间构建了一个小型 Chrome 扩展（Quill - 五个书写工具，仅限设备上的 AI）作为测试平台。 扩展本身并不是真正的重点；我用它来找出基于用户机器而不是您机器上的模型进行构建的实际不同之处。 这些是我在开始之前会告诉其他开发人员的事情。

不存在一个 API — 有一个任务 API 和一个后备 API，并且您两者都需要

Chrome 公开了两层：专用任务 API（重写器、校对器、摘要器、编写器），每个 API 都围绕一项具有类型化选项的作业，以及通用语言模型（“提示 API”），您可以使用原始系统提示符来驱动它。 它们由单独的 chrome://flags 条目进行控制，并独立稳定地推出 - 这意味着在任何给定的真实机器上，它们的某些子集已启用，而其余的则未启用。

仅针对 Rewriter 进行硬编码意味着该功能会在仅打开 Prompt API 的任何配置文件上静默停止工作，反之亦然。 修复方法是每个功能都有一个后备链：尝试专用 API，如果它不可用，则重建与 LanguageModel 的系统提示相同的任务。

async function doRewrite(sharedContext, text, lengthPref, variationHint) {
  const a = await avail('Rewriter');
  if (a !== 'unavailable') {
    const r = await Rewriter.create({ sharedContext, tone: 'as-is', length: lengthPref });
    try { return await r.rewrite(text, { context: variationHint }); }
    finally { r.destroy(); }
  }
  // Rewriter isn't enabled on this machine — do the same job via the Prompt API.
  const sys = `${sharedContext} Preserve the original meaning. Output only the rewritten text.`;
  return promptOnce(variationHint ? `${sys} ${variationHint}` : sys, text);
}

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

这大约使每个功能的代码增加了一倍，这就是“在我的机器上运行”和“在我的机器上运行”之间的区别。 将“任务 API 已启用”视为您检查的运行时事实，而不是构建时假设。

“不可用”是一个状态机，而不是布尔值

availability() 不返回 true/false — 它返回不可用、可下载、正在下载或可用。 当我第一次在明显打开标志的干净配置文件上点击不可用时，我认为这意味着确实无法从内容脚本的隔离世界访问 API，并开始构建一个离屏文档中继来解决权限边界，结果证明该边界并不存在。 实际原因：我切换了标志但从未重新启动浏览器，因此 Chrome 仍以旧标志状态运行。 不可用和“您忘记重新启动”会产生相同的字符串。

一旦您实际上处于可下载或正在下载状态，create() 就会采用一个监视器回调来报告下载进度 - 对新启用的 API 的第一次调用会触发一次性模型拉取，这可能需要一段时间，如果您不将其连接起来，您的 UI 看起来就会挂起：

const opts = { sharedContext, tone: 'as-is' };
if (a !== 'available') {
  opts.monitor = (m) => m.addEventListener('downloadprogress', e => {
    showProgress(`Downloading model… ${Math.round(e.loaded * 100)}%`);
  });
}
const r = await Rewriter.create(opts);

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

两个要点：在确认“不可用”不仅仅是未翻转的标志或待下载之前，不要构建解决方法，并且始终假设全新安装的第一个实际调用会很慢。

该模型足够小，说明不能保证

与您习惯通过 HTTP 调用的模型相比，任务 API 和提示 API 都运行一个真正小的模型。 具体来说，这意味着：

  • 长度和计数说明为近似值。 要求“恰好一个句子”或“少于 200 个字符”，您会得到接近但不准确的结果，常常导致您无法在硬限制很重要的情况下显示原始输出 - 在代码中对其进行测量并将模型的尝试视为初稿，而不是基本事实。
  • “给我三个不同的版本”低估了它们的相似程度。 请求 3 个变体的单个调用往往会通过简单的单词交换返回同一想法的 3 个变体。 更好的结果来自三个单独的调用，每个调用都有明确的指示，以倾向不同的方向（更短、更正式、更随意）——将变化视为您通过提示设计的东西，而不是您作为参数请求的东西。
  • 输出语言不是隐式的。 LanguageModel 和任务 API 都接受带有语言数组的预期输入/预期输出。 跳过它，你会得到一个控制台警告和（根据 Chrome 自己的文档）不太可靠的输出证明 - 这是一个很容易被忽略的单行修复，因为没有它就没有任何问题，它只会变得更糟。
const opts = {
  initialPrompts: [{ role: 'system', content: systemPrompt }],
  expectedInputs: [{ type: 'text', languages: ['en'] }],
  expectedOutputs: [{ type: 'text', languages: ['en'] }],
};

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

您无法构建假设模型存在的 CI 管道

GitHub Actions 运行程序上没有 Gemini Nano，今天您的笔记本电脑上也可能没有，除非您已经下载了它。 如果您的测试套件需要真实模型，那么您就没有测试套件 - 您有一个手动 QA 检查表。 获得真正覆盖的唯一方法是将 Rewriter / Summarizer / LanguageModel 等模拟为 jsdom 全局变量，并针对模拟运行实际的、未修改的功能代码。

这是有效的，有一个尖锐的优势：你的模拟必须像真实模型一样改变它的输出，否则它会隐藏错误而不是捕获它们。 我有一个音调变体生成器，可以删除几乎相同的输出（一个合法的防重复防护），并针对一个模拟进行测试，该模拟返回相同的固定字符串，无论使用哪个提示调用它。 每一个“生成 3 个变体”测试都通过了——只有一个结果，因为模拟使所有三个都崩溃到同一个重复数据删除密钥，并且断言检查“至少得到一个结果”没有注意到它得到了错误的数字。 模拟的懒惰恰好产生了看起来像是成功的输出。 一旦模拟改变了每个输入的响应（与任何真实的生成模型相同），测试就会开始失败，直到重复数据删除逻辑实际上是正确的。

一般规则：如果一个功能扇出为 N 个不同的事物，那么您的模拟必须能够返回 N 个不同的事物，否则您就不会测试扇出 - 您正在测试您的代码不会崩溃。

浏览器边界是真实的，即使你的第一次猜测是错误的

内容脚本（在您实际操作的页面上运行的代码）无法将选项卡导航到 chrome:// URL；这是 Chrome 的政策，而不是可以绕过的错误。 如果您的用户体验的一部分涉及将某人发送到 chrome://flags 来启用某项功能，则必须通过 chrome.tabs.create 或类似的方式从扩展程序的后台服务工作者请求该导航，并由内容脚本中的消息触发。

// content script: can't navigate to chrome:// itself
chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ type: 'OPEN_FLAGS', flag: 'rewriter-api-for-gemini-nano' });

// background worker: can
chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener((msg) => {
  if (msg.type === 'OPEN_FLAGS') {
    chrome.tabs.create({ url: `chrome://flags/#${msg.flag}` });
  }
});

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

这是我正确怀疑存在的边界 - 之前的错误是假设可用性检查具有类似的边界，但事实并非如此。 这两部分都值得内化：对页面级脚本可以执行的操作的一些限制是真实且永久的，解决方法是将操作交给更有特权的上下文。 其他一些则只是转移注意力，仅从错误消息来看就完全一样。 区分它们的唯一方法是在构建解决方法之前隔离实际原因。

这些都不是 Quill 特有的——它是在 Chrome 的设备上 AI 上构建任何东西实际涉及的内容，与功能的作用无关。

如果你想看看这些结果是如何产生的：Quill 是五个写作工具（重写、校对、总结、列表/表格、自由格式撰写），完全在 Gemini Nano 上运行，在 Chrome 网上应用店免费。
